The Post (2017)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $81,903,458Details
International Box Office $97,865,999
Worldwide Box Office $179,769,457
Domestic Physical Disc Sales
DVD Sales (estimated) $2,993,797
Blu-ray Sales (estimated) $3,663,131
Total $6,656,928
Further financial details...

Synopsis

A drama about the unlikely partnership of Katharine Graham, the first female publisher of The Washington Post, and its driven editor Ben Bradlee, as they race to catch up with The New York Times to expose a massive cover-up of government secrets that spanned three decades and four U.S. Presidents. The two must overcome their differences as they risk their careers—and their very freedom—to bring long-buried truths to light.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$526,011 (0.6% of total gross)
Legs:4.23 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:45.6% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$50,000,000 (worldwide box office is 3.6 times production budget)
Theater counts:9 opening theaters/2,851 max. theaters, 5.6 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $107,562,451

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: December 22nd, 2017 (Limited) by 20th Century Fox
January 12th, 2018 (Expands Wide) by 20th Century Fox
International Releases: January 12th, 2018 (Wide) (Australia)
January 12th, 2018 (Wide) (Turkey)
January 12th, 2018 (Wide), released as Post, The (New Zealand)
January 12th, 2018 (Wide) (India)
January 18th, 2018 (Wide) (Singapore)
Video Release: April 3rd, 2018 by Fox Home Entertainment
MPA Rating: PG-13 for language and brief war violence.
(Rating bulletin 2503 (Cert #51311), 11/22/2017)
Running Time: 115 minutes
Award nomination: 2018 Oscars Best Picture Nominee
Plot point: Cover Up, Government Corruption, Investigative Journalist, News, Political
Social setting: Newspaper Industry, Vietnam War
Subgenre: Docudrama
Source:Based on Real Life Events
Genre:Drama
Production Method:Live Action
Creative Type:Dramatization
Production/Financing Companies: DreamWorks Pictures, Amblin Entertainment, 20th Century Fox, Participant Media, Star Thrower Entertainment
Production Countries: United States
Languages: English

Synopsis

A drama about the unlikely partnership of Katharine Graham, the first female publisher of The Washington Post, and its driven editor Ben Bradlee, as they race to catch up with The New York Times to expose a massive cover-up of government secrets that spanned three decades and four U.S. Presidents. The two must overcome their differences as they risk their careers—and their very freedom—to bring long-buried truths to light.
